title: User Authentication API with FastAPI
summary: A REST API built using FastAPI that allows users to register and log in with JWT-based authentication and password hashing for secure user management.

user_stories.title: Register a new user
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to register with the system so that I can access the application.
acceptance_criteria: The system provides an endpoint for user registration
The user must provide a valid email and password
The password is hashed before being stored in the database
A successful registration returns a 201 status code
Invalid email or password formats return a 400 status code

user_stories.title: Login with existing user credentials
user_stories.description: As a registered user, I want to log in with my email and password so that I can access the application.
acceptance_criteria: The system provides an endpoint for user login
The user must provide a valid email and password
The system verifies the credentials against the stored hash
On successful login, a JWT token is returned
Invalid credentials return a 401 status code

user_stories.title: Access protected endpoints with JWT
user_stories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to access protected endpoints using a valid JWT token.
acceptance_criteria: The system provides protected endpoints that require a valid JWT token
Endpoints return a 401 status code if no token is provided
Endpoints return a 403 status code if the token is invalid or expired

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Passwords must be hashed using a strong algorithm (e.g., bcrypt)
JWT tokens must have an expiration time and be signed securely
API endpoints must respond within 500ms under normal load
The system should support horizontal scaling for increased traffic
The system must handle at least 99.9% uptime

constraints: The system must use Python and FastAPI as the primary framework
Database must be relational (e.g., PostgreSQL or SQLite)
Password hashing must be implemented using a well-known library (e.g., bcrypt)
assumptions: The user will have access to a database server
The system will be deployed on a cloud platform (e.g., AWS, Heroku)
The JWT secret key will be managed securely (e.g., via environment variables)
out_of_scope: Email verification or password reset functionality
User profile management beyond basic registration and login
tech_stack_preferences: FastAPI for building the REST API
PostgreSQL or SQLite for the database
